SECTION 1  
A squirrel is sitting in front of the second hollow. It says: "There are no nuts in the other hollow." If this squirrel is a red squirrel, it would be telling the truth, so there are no nuts in the first hollow. However, the second squirrel says: "There is at least one nut in one of the hollows." If this squirrel is a red squirrel, it would be telling the truth, so there is at least one nut in one of the hollows. But if the squirrel is gray, it is lying, so there are no nuts in the other hollow. This leads to a contradiction if the first squirrel is red. Therefore, the first squirrel must be gray, and the second squirrel is red.

SECTION 2  
The first squirrel is gray, so it is lying. That means there are nuts in the other hollow. The second squirrel is red, so it is telling the truth. That means there is at least one nut in one of the hollows. This is consistent with the scenario where there are nuts in the first hollow. Thus, the squirrel in front of the second hollow is red, and the squirrel in the first hollow is gray.
